title: Publisher Buffers, Drain, Admission, and Bounded Batching · Contract Lens: Drain Single Waiter
summary: A publisher awaits a boolean from publish, ignores false, buffers unbounded work during broker flow control, and batches until memory exhaustion while upstream requests continue to receive success. Planned artifact: Add and verify the drain single waiter increment in publisher admission and buffer-pressure controller. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks.
Treat amqplib publish and sendToQueue return values as writable-buffer admission signals rather than delivery confirmations or Promises.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Drain Single Waiter · Theory Drain Single Waiter
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9594 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to propagate blocked, byte capacity, publish boolean, and drain state into upstream admission; keep reconnection and retry work cancellable under one deadline; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Drain Single Waiter contract for the supplied data.
